verdict_reason: Both disagreements (moha-kalilam; nirvedam śrotavyasya śrutasya ca) carry full explanation paragraphs. The moha-kalilam entry correctly distinguishes Prabhupada/Mukundananda's spatial rendering (forest, quagmire) from Shankara's cognitive reading (aviveka) from Ramanuja's ethical-soteriological reading (impurity of sin). The nirvedam entry correctly identifies the SCOPED nature of indifference — all four traditions agree the indifference is to a specific class of content (phala-promising teachings), not generic disengagement. The engineering-layer constraint ('not teaching disengagement from all new inputs') is named. Both substantive. Pass.
